document.summary: Mozambique is experiencing a complex humanitarian crisis due to climate-related shocks and armed conflict, severely impacting vulnerable populations' health. In 2026, floods in central and southern provinces have led to displacement and increased risks of waterborne diseases. Insecurity in Cabo Delgado continues to strain health systems, necessitating urgent humanitarian assistance. Health Cluster partners are coordinating responses to deliver essential health services, despite significant challenges in access and resources.
key_takeaways: Mozambique faces a humanitarian crisis from climate shocks and armed conflict.
Floods in 2026 have caused displacement and health infrastructure damage.
Cabo Delgado's insecurity exacerbates health system strains.
High humanitarian needs prioritize primary health care and emergency services.
Health Cluster partners are implementing integrated health service responses.
Challenges include access, resource availability, and monitoring mechanisms.
document.why_it_matters: The report highlights the urgent health needs in Mozambique, emphasizing the importance of coordinated humanitarian responses to support vulnerable populations affected by both natural disasters and conflict.
